skill.name: managing-project-rules
skill.description: Creates and updates modular Claude Code project rule files in the .claude/rules directory.
skill.summary: Manage focused Claude Code project rules with clear scope, filenames, and frontmatter.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, filesystem, and weak cryptography patterns, but these are markdown examples and inline code references. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection text were found in the reviewed files. The main publishable risk is that the skill intentionally writes Claude project memory files, which can influence future AI behavior.

medium_findings.title: Writes Claude Project Memory Rules
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for filesystem modification. The skill instructs the assistant to create or update files in .claude/rules/. This is the intended purpose, but those files are project memory and can affect future Claude Code behavior. Users should review generated rules before committing or sharing them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly directs creation or updates under .claude/rules/. The impact is limited to local project instruction files, so this is elevated but not malicious.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Spans Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for most external command detections. Backticks in SKILL.md and the reference document are markdown formatting for paths, filenames, and examples, not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are markdown documentation with fenced examples and inline paths. No executable source file or runtime command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Symlink and Home Directory References Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for hidden home directory and symlink alerts. The reference text documents supported Claude rule locations and optional symlink examples. It does not execute ln or access hidden files by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious lines are explanatory markdown and fenced bash examples. They could guide a user action, but there is no automation or stealth access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Filename False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for weak cryptography detections. The flagged lines contain markdown filenames such as testing.md and api-design.md, where md is a file extension, not a digest algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is markdown documentation about rule filenames. There is no hashing, cryptographic API, or security decision based on MD5 or SHA-1.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose project guidance inside one large instruction file. This skill organizes Claude Code rules into focused files with clear scope and naming.
actual_capabilities: Creates new markdown rule files under .claude/rules/.
Updates existing project rule files while preserving useful content.
Organizes rules by topic, directory, and filename.
Adds YAML frontmatter for path-specific rule scopes.
Checks rule files for duplicate or conflicting guidance.
limitations: It does not validate whether Claude Code loads rules in a specific product version.
It cannot guarantee rule quality without accurate project context.
It should not rewrite broad rule structures without user approval.
It does not provide a security policy review beyond rule organization.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Coding Guidance
use_cases.description: Create focused rules for style, testing, reviews, and security so contributors receive consistent AI guidance.

use_cases.title: Split a Large CLAUDE.md File
use_cases.description: Move broad project instructions into smaller topic files that are easier to maintain and review.

use_cases.title: Scope Rules to Specific Paths
use_cases.description: Add frontmatter so frontend, backend, or test guidance applies only to matching files.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Rule
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one new topic rule.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Claude Code rule for our testing conventions in .claude/rules/. Keep it focused and easy to review.

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve current project guidance without losing prior decisions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the existing .claude/rules/ files and update the code style guidance. Preserve useful content and remove duplicates.

prompt_templates.title: Add Path-Specific Guidance
prompt_templates.scenario: Apply rules only to a subset of the repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a path-specific rule for src/api/**/*.ts that covers validation, error responses, and API documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Reorganize Rule Structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced cleanup for a growing repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit our Claude Code rules and propose a cleaner directory structure by topic. Ask before making major structural changes.

output_examples.input: Create rules for React component development.
output_examples.output: A focused frontend rule file is created with component naming, state handling, styling, and test expectations.

output_examples.input: Update our testing rules and avoid duplicates.
output_examples.output: The existing testing guidance is consolidated, repeated points are removed, and the final file location is reported.

output_examples.input: Make backend API rules apply only to API files.
output_examples.output: A scoped backend rule is prepared with path frontmatter and concise API development requirements.

best_practices: Keep each rule file focused on one topic.
Review generated rules before committing them to the repository.
Use path-specific frontmatter only when a rule truly has limited scope.
anti_patterns: Do not store secrets, credentials, or private operational details in rule files.
Do not create broad rules that conflict with existing project guidance.
Do not add symlinked rule sources unless the team trusts and reviews them.

faq.question: What does this skill manage?
faq.answer: It manages markdown rule files for Claude Code under the .claude/rules directory.

faq.question: Does it replace CLAUDE.md?
faq.answer: No. It helps split project guidance into focused rule files that can work alongside CLAUDE.md.

faq.question: Can it create path-specific rules?
faq.answer: Yes. It can add YAML frontmatter with paths so rules apply to selected files.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No executable commands are included in the skill. The reference file contains markdown examples only.

faq.question: What should users review?
faq.answer: Users should review generated rule text, scope, filenames, and any references to shared or symlinked rules.

faq.question: Is it safe for team repositories?
faq.answer: It is publishable with review. Rule files can influence future AI behavior, so teams should approve changes.
